figures.caption: FIG. 5. Cumulative probability of absolute ranging residuals for UWB, WiFi, and BLE.

FIG. 1. The measurement plate equipped with all sensors. The platform is used in static mode: it is moved manually between reference points and remains stationary with a fixed orientation for 3 min at each point while all sensors record in parallel. No data is collected during transit.

FIG. 2. 2D locations of measurement points inside and outside the hall shown on a geo-referenced aerial image (North-up). The arrows indicate the orientation of the local Cartesian coordinate frame. Base imagery ©2025 Google Earth. [27]

FIG. 3. Layout map showing measurement grid (A1-A13, B1-B6, T1-T6) with WiFi, UWB, and BLE anchor points overlaid on test site in the local Cartesian coordinate frame. Note that the axis orientation differs from Fig. 2 as the two figures represent different coordinate reference systems (see Table 2).
FIG. 4. Processing workflow from measurements to processed output.
